items.title: Companies Act 71 of 2008: section 22
items.text: Section 22 prohibits carrying on business recklessly, with gross negligence, with intent to defraud, or for a fraudulent purpose.

items.title: Companies Act 71 of 2008: section 26
items.text: Section 26 addresses access to company records and is relevant where the sealed record shows a member or stakeholder being blocked from records needed to protect legal or governance rights.

items.title: Companies Act 71 of 2008: section 163
items.text: Section 163 provides relief from oppressive or prejudicial conduct and is the primary company-law hook when exclusionary or unfairly prejudicial governance conduct is grounded in the record.

items.title: Cybercrimes Act 19 of 2020
items.text: Primary South African cybercrime statute for unlawful access, interference, password misuse, cyber fraud, cyber forgery, cyber extortion, and related offences.

items.title: Prevention and Combating of Corrupt Activities Act 12 of 2004: section 3
items.text: Section 3 creates the general offence of corruption and matters when a sealed record supports a gratification-based or inducement-based corruption theory rather than a pure governance complaint.
